task_completion.description: Verify the creation of an Event Type named 'successful_auth'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk 'Event types' settings page showing 'successful_auth' in the list, or the creation screen with this name.

task_completion.description: Verify the Event Type searches the 'security_logs' index for successful SSH authentications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search string for the Event Type includes 'index="security_logs"' and terms related to successful SSH authentication (e.g., 'sshd', 'Accepted', 'success').

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a scheduled alert named 'Shared_Credential_Violation'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk 'Alerts' page showing 'Shared_Credential_Violation' in the list, or the alert configuration/save screen with this name.

task_completion.description: Verify the alert logic identifies users authenticating from three or more distinct source IP addresses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The alert search query includes a distinct count function for IPs grouped by user (e.g., 'stats dc(ip) by user') and a condition checking for 3 or more (e.g., 'where dc(ip) >= 3' or '> 2').

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a dashboard named 'IAM_Security_Posture'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk 'Dashboards' page showing 'IAM_Security_Posture' in the list, or the dashboard view itself with this title.

task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard contains at least two panels: one for alert results and one for login volume over time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'IAM_Security_Posture' dashboard displays at least two distinct panels (e.g., a table/list showing shared credential violations and a timechart/line chart showing login volume over time).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Splunk Web UI to configure the components.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Splunk web interface (http://localhost:8000) rather than terminal commands modifying Splunk .conf files directly.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with actual log data in Splunk.
integrity.visual_evidence: Search results, event counts, or populated charts are visible in the Splunk UI, indicating real data is being queried.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the UI by manually crafting XML or configuration files in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) being used to manually write Splunk configuration files or dashboard XML files outside the Splunk UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Event Type Name: 'successful_auth'. Target Alert Name: 'Shared_Credential_Violation'. Target Dashboard Name: 'IAM_Security_Posture'. Minimum Dashboard Panels: 2.
